headline: Influencer Launches 'Anti-Influencer' Movement, Gains 47 Million Followers While Criticizing Followers
summary: The 'Authentically Fake' movement promises to be the most authentic account while maintaining 47 million followers.
fullBody: Social media influencer Tammy Contradiction has launched the 'Authentically Fake' movement, a anti-influencer influencer account that criticizes influencers while simultaneously being an influencer with 47 million followers. Her account posts content like 'I'm so tired of fake people on social media, anyway here's my sponsored content for this protein powder.' and 'I hate flexing but look at this exotic vacation I'm on, courtesy of a brand deal.' The movement has been praised as 'the most honest thing on the internet' and 'deeply concerning.' Tammy says her mission is to 'prove that you can be authentic by being fake about your authenticity.' Sponsors are reportedly fighting to work with her because 'she's reaching people who are sick of being reached.' The next phase of the movement involves launching an anti-brand brand that sells anti-merchandise.
imagePrompt: Influencer holding phone showing follower count, text saying 47 MILLION, with a confused expression, comic illustration, flat design, pink and black colors
